name: Hejazi Arabic
notes: Hejazi Arabic (Ў≠ЎђЎІЎ≤ўК), urban western Saudi Arabia: Mecca, Medina, Jeddah. References Omar (1975) and Abdoh (2010). DEFINING FEATURES modeled here: (1) qaf ўВ вЖТ /…°/ (voiced velar stop) in inherited vocabulary вАФ part of the early Hejazi /q/вЖТ/…°/ shift; /q/ retained only in learned/scientific/religious borrowings (older descriptions positing a / Ф/ reflex are a misattribution of the Levantine/Egyptian feature); (2) jim Ўђ вЖТ /d Т/ (affricate; [ Т] for some speakers); (3) interdentals MERGED TO STOPS in the urban koine вАФ ЎЂ вЖТ /t/ (~/s/), Ў∞ вЖТ /d/ (~/z/), ЎЄ вЖТ /dЋ§/ (~/zЋ§/); interdentals are retained only in Bedouin/rural speech; (4) MONOPHTHONGIZATION вАФ Classical /aj aw/ вЖТ [eЋР oЋР] (baytвЖТbeЋРt, yawmвЖТjoЋРm), declared as allophone_rules HEJ_MONO_* (Omar 1975; Abdoh 2010), giving the 8-vowel system /a u i; aЋР uЋР iЋР eЋР oЋР/; (5) four emphatics /sЋ§ dЋ§ tЋ§ zЋ§/ with pharyngealization spreading that backs low vowels вАФ inherited from the ar-x-peninsular parent (AR_PEN_EMPH_*). No kвЖТ[t Г] Gulf/Najdi affrication. Cosmopolitan koine shaped by Hajj contact. INPUT CONTRACT: transcribes orthography+harakat; short vowels surface only when the input is vocalised (dialectal Arabic is normally written defectively).

sources.author: Omar, M.K.
sources.title: Saudi Arabic, Urban Hijazi Dialect: A Basic Course
sources.notes: Urban Hijazi phonemic inventory: qafвЖТ/…°/, jimвЖТ/d Т/, interdentals merged to stops, 8-vowel system, monophthongization of /aj aw/ вЖТ /eЋР oЋР/.

sources.author: Abdoh, E.
sources.title: A Study of the Phonology and Morphology of Urban Meccan Arabic (PhD dissertation)
sources.notes: Urban Meccan/Hejazi vowel chart; 8-vowel system with /eЋР oЋР/ from monophthongized diphthongs; four emphatics /sЋ§ dЋ§ tЋ§ zЋ§/.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties

allophone_rules.notes: Urban Hejazi monophthongization (Omar 1975; Abdoh 2010): the Classical diphthong /aj/ is realised [eЋР], e.g. bayt вЖТ [beЋРt]. Yields the 8-vowel system /a u i aЋР uЋР iЋР eЋР oЋР/.
Urban Hejazi monophthongization (Omar 1975; Abdoh 2010): the Classical diphthong /aw/ is realised [oЋР], e.g. yawm вЖТ [joЋРm].

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Hijazi Arabic.

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, ¬І2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVЋРC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiЋИtaЋРb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVЋР/CVC) does (muЋИdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ЋИmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant вАФ a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
